prose:
Fukui reactivity descriptors for acrolein were computed using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier standard, density-fit) in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. The reference neutral, cation (+1, doublet), and anion (−1, doublet) structures were all converged. Mulliken partial charges were extracted for all three species, and Fukui indices f⁺ (electrophilic), f⁻ (nucleophilic), f⁰ (radical), and dual descriptor Δf were computed. The terminal alkene carbon (C4) is the most electrophilic site (f⁺ = 0.642, Δf = 0.555), consistent with Michael-addition reactivity for an α,β-unsaturated aldehyde. The carbonyl oxygen is the most nucleophilic site (f⁻ = 0.394). SCF convergence and charge conservation checks (sum f⁺ = 1.0, sum f⁻ = 1.0) both passed. A reactivity plot was generated. All integrity checks passed (trustworthy: true).</prose>
